title: Pairwise effect sizes: body mass, video gaming, physical activity (10 studies)
description: Direct-information MASEMiner extraction of pairwise effect sizes between body-mass measures (BMI, fat mass, waist), video-game use, and physical activity, from 10 primary studies. Sample-level metadata (sample size, country, mean age, % female, clinical status) is included. Extracted with the Marker 2022 prompt and gpt-5.5 — provided as the first worked example for the metalens-datasets repository.

authors.name: Caroline Marker
authors.affiliation: Human-Computer-Media Institute, University of Würzburg, Germany

authors.name: Timo Gnambs
authors.affiliation: Johannes Kepler University Linz, Austria; Leibniz Institute for Educational Trajectories, Bamberg, Germany

authors.name: Markus Appel
authors.affiliation: Human-Computer-Media Institute, University of Würzburg, Germany

verification.notes: Raw model output — no human review performed.  All 10 input JSON exports had empty human_overrides arrays.

related_paper.citation: Marker, C., Gnambs, T., & Appel, M. (2022). Exploring the myth of the chubby gamer: A meta-analysis on sedentary video gaming and body mass. Social Science & Medicine, 301, Article 112325.
